timeline.label: Q3 FY2026 10-Q: nine-month operating cash flow $934.8M; cash $1.72B; total long-term debt $8.87B
FY2025 10-K filed: first full-year GAAP profitability; revenue $3.22B; OCF $793.9M; net income $985.3M
S-3ASR shelf registration renewed; equity and convertible issuance capacity for growth funding
FY2024 10-K: revenue $2.32B; path toward operating leverage as GMV scales
Affirm Card and debit wallet expansion accelerate; platform shifts from pure BNPL checkout to broader consumer payments
FY2023 10-K: revenue $1.59B; credit normalization post-pandemic BNPL boom
FY2022 10-K amid rate hikes: funding cost pressure and credit loss normalization begin
Affirm completes SPAC merger and begins public reporting as AFRM on Nasdaq
Affirm IPO via SPAC (AFG/Atlas); merchant network includes Amazon, Shopify, and major retailers
Affirm founded by Max Levchin; buy-now-pay-later model targets transparent installment lending at checkout

industry.structure: U.S. buy-now-pay-later and consumer installment lending is fragmented among Affirm, Block (Afterpay), PayPal Pay in 4, Klarna, and card-network installment plans; merchant integration and underwriting data create modest switching costs but low formal barriers to entry.
industry.share_shift: Affirm gained share through Amazon, Shopify, and Apple partnerships while pure-play BNPL peers consolidated; card issuers and wallets embed installment features at checkout.
industry.trend: Regulatory scrutiny of late fees and disclosure, rising funding costs versus 2020–2021, and expansion into debit cards and broader wallet use cases reshape economics from growth-at-all-costs toward profitable scale.
